item.title: 如果从头开始，您会选择哪些工具来创建个人作品集网站？ | What tools would you choose to create a personal portfolio site if starting from scratch?
item.description: 原文:
item.author: ben

item.title: 不要将 100vh 用于移动响应 | Don't use 100vh for mobile responsive
item.description:
通常，我们使用height:100vh进行全屏布局，这很容易获得更好的设计。

例子

.content { height : 100vh ; }

但是当我们在实际设备上测试我们的设计时，我们会遇到几个问题：

  • 大多数移动设备上的 Chrome 和 Firefox 浏览器在顶部都有一个 UI（地址栏等）。
  • 在 Safari 上，更棘手的地址栏位于底部。
  • 不同的浏览器有不同大小的视口
  • 移动设备计算浏览器视口为（顶栏 + 文档 + 底栏）= 100vh
  • 使用 100vh 将整个文档填充到页面

问题

已检测到滚动条问题。 糟糕的用户流和难以浏览的内容。

注意：我还在 safari 上测试过这个问题，这会导致更糟糕的用户流量

解决方案

使用JS检测app的高度

使用window.innerheight属性设置页面的高度（使用 javascript）。

 const documentHeight = () => { const doc = document . documentElement doc . style . setProperty ( ' --doc-height ' , ` ${ window . innerHeight } px` ) } window . addEventListener ( ' resize ' , documentHeight ) documentHeight ()

使用，CSS 变量

:root { --doc-height : 100% ; } html , body { padding : 0 ; margin : 0 ; height : 100vh ; /* fallback for Js load */ height : var ( --doc-height ); }

在这里，documentHeight 函数设置新的样式属性 var('--doc-height') 并包含当前窗口高度。

最终结果

注意：现在没有任何垂直的额外滚动条出现在 Safari 上也没有问题。 结论
所以，我建议你尝试一下你的项目并享受它！

如果您有任何问题或疑问，请随时分享您的想法和意见，并给我留言。

直到那时，
继续黑客攻击，干杯



原文:

Generally, we use height:100vh for fullscreen layout which is easy hack and convenient way to get better design.

Example 

.content { height: 100vh;
}

But when we test our design on actual device, we encounter several issues:

  • Mostly Chrome and Firefox browsers on mobile have got a UI (address bar etc) at the top.
  • On Safari it get's more tricky address bar is at the bottom.
  • Different browsers have different sized viewports
  • Mobile devices calc browser viewport as (top bar + document + bottom bar) = 100vh
  • Whole document is filled to the page using 100vh

Problems

Scrollbar issues has been detected. Bad user flow and difficult to navigate the content.

Note: I have also tested this issues on safari which makes more bad user flow

Solutions

Detect the height of the app by using JS

Setting the height of the page (using javascript) with the window.innerheight property.

const documentHeight = () => { const doc = document.documentElement doc.style.setProperty('--doc-height', `${window.innerHeight}px`)
}
window.addEventListener(‘resize’, documentHeight)
documentHeight()

Using, CSS Variable 

:root { --doc-height: 100%;
} html,
body { padding: 0; margin: 0; height: 100vh; /* fallback for Js load */ height: var(--doc-height);
}

Here, documentHeight function sets new style property var('--doc-height') and includes current window height.

Final Results

Chrome Browser

Note: There is no any vertical extra scrollbar is appearing now also no issues on Safari too. The bottom address bar of safari is always on the bottom which makes good user flow to the website

Conclusion
👏👏 By coming this far I hope you can solve the mobile devices viewport issues. So, I suggest you give it a try on your project and enjoy it!

Feel free to share your thoughts and opinions and leave me a comment if you have any problems or questions.

Till then,
Keep on Hacking, Cheers
item.author: nirazanbasnet

item.title: 分享 ️Love ️ — 你最喜欢 DEV 上的#beginners 帖子是什么？ | Share the ️Love ️— What's your favorite #beginners post on DEV?
item.description:
几周前，我写了一篇文章，名为What's your 最喜爱的 DEV post？（由其他人！）并且你们都加入了对话，删除了很多优秀帖子的链接，以及对创建这些帖子的作者的一些非常友好的话。

所以，我想我们不妨再试一次。 这一次，我很想知道：你最喜欢在#beginners下分享的帖子是什么？

注意：您可以通过以下方式将帖子嵌入评论：

{% embed dev.to/username/name-of-post %}

当你分享他们的作品时，请考虑给作者一个@mention。 这可能会让他们感觉良好，我想我们都会欣赏这一点。 所以，去找任何你想强调和分享爱的#beginners 帖子吧！ ❤️ ❤️



原文:

A few weeks back I wrote up a post called What's your favorite post on DEV? (by someone else!) and you all chimed into the conversation dropping so many links to excellent posts, plus some really kind words for the authors that created those posts.

So, I thought we might as well try this again. 😀

This time, I'm curious to know: what's your favorite post shared under #beginners?

Note: you can embed a post into a comment by writing:

{% embed dev.to/username/name-of-post %}

Please consider giving the author an @mention when ya share their work. It's probably gonna make them feel good and I think we can all appreciate that. 🙌

So, go find whatever #beginners post you'd like to highlight and share the love! ❤️
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 将您的博客文章变成虚拟演讲 | Turn Your Blog Post Into A Virtual Talk
item.description:
你好 Codenewbies 👋，

去年 1 月，我报名参加了CodeNewbie 的 2022 年挑战赛：公开学习。 这个为期五周的挑战教会了我如何通过内容创作在公共场合学习，从制定学习计划到执行。
他们鼓励我们撰写一系列博客文章，然后将它们转化为音频/视频 (A/V) 内容，例如创建视频或播客或提交演讲。

公开演讲和 A/V 内容——基本上任何需要我露面和与公众交谈的内容——都是我的氪石。
我计划走出我的舒适区并尝试一下。 但它没有发生🙈。

背景

在 CodeNewbie 的挑战期间，Virtual Coffee 计划了闪电会谈。 这是一年一度的内部成员活动，每位参与成员都会就技术相关话题进行 10 分钟的讨论。
和往年一样，除了做啦啦队长，我没有任何参加这个演讲的计划。

我确实有一些公开演讲的经验。 我是Virtual Coffee 播客一集中的嘉宾。 然后，我参加了Virtual Coffee 的午餐和学习活动，与另外两名成员一起作为演讲者介绍了 The Collab Lab 。 但在这些活动中，我并不是独自一人。 独唱对我来说肯定更具挑战性。

但是我很想尝试一下。 演讲的听众是我们的会员。 所以我想，“嘿，没关系。这就像和我的朋友聊天，但更正式。如果这不起作用，至少我试过了。”

好吧，我说话了！ 在这篇文章中，我想分享如何将你的博客文章变成基于这种体验的虚拟演讲。
如果您打算第一次进行虚拟演讲，我希望它可以帮助您！ 搜索主题

我联系了 Bekah，告诉她我有一个想法要做闪电演讲，但我不知道我可以分享什么话题。 我还没有准备好谈论一些技术性的东西。 她鼓励我去做，并建议谈论一些我最了解且不需要技术的东西——一些我亲身经历过的技术。

我意识到，通过我的经验，我最了解的与成为一名自学成才的开发人员和技术博主有关。

我浏览了我的博客文章并一一阅读。
我最终选择了我的一篇文章， 《新自学编程新手的 10 条提示》 。 它不仅来自我自己的经历，而且后来，这篇文章对我来说变得很特别。 有人联系我，告诉我他们会重读这篇文章，以便在他们感到沮丧时给予他们力量。 几个月后，他们在科技领域找到了第一份工作！ 我非常感谢他们让我知道这件事。 它让我想起了我为什么写作； 作为我自己和帮助他人的笔记。

接下来我知道了，我提交了闪电谈话的表格。

把那篇博客文章变成演讲

写大纲

编写大纲与编写脚本不同。 在这一点上，突出显示你想谈论的事情。 概述重要的事情并记住留在主题内。 有要点会很有帮助。

创建幻灯片

那里有很多不错的免费幻灯片模板。 但是我使用Canva来创建我的，因为它有很多可爱的设计，即使是免费版本。 把幻灯片当作一种工具，让观众了解你在说什么。

不要在幻灯片中写长句子。 我们希望观众关注并从我们分享的内容中获得价值，而不是通过阅读幻灯片来分散他们的注意力。

编写脚本

创建幻灯片后，现在是编写脚本的时候了。 对于每张幻灯片，逐字逐句地写下你想说的话。 想想讲故事。 尝试将一个点连接到另一个点，一张幻灯片连接到另一个，让它们成为一个大故事。

考虑时间和谈话的方式

会有问答吗？ 如果有，问答会在演讲期间还是之后进行？ 如果不清楚，您可能想向委员会询问。

就我而言，每个闪电演讲者都有 10 分钟的时间，没有问答环节。 所以，我准备了 8 分钟的演讲，并给自己 2 分钟的缓冲时间，以防万一。

排练时间

你知道吗？ 即使是多年演讲的人也会感到焦虑。 这就是你想要排练的原因。 排练并不意味着背诵。 通过排练，您将习惯于看幻灯片。 然后你会熟悉下一张幻灯片以及你会说什么。

你练习得越多，你听起来就越自然，因为你感觉更舒服。

排练时录制自己会很有帮助。 起初我不想这样做。 看着和听自己会很奇怪😅。 但最后，我做到了。 我需要知道我如何呈现我的演讲。 我从项目委员会那里听说他们使用 Zoom 来举办活动。 所以我用它来练习和记录自己。

演讲前我给自己录了 12 遍😆。

您可以通过录制自己来跟踪您的时间并聆听您的单词和语调。 注意你的肢体语言、光线和在镜头前的位置。
看到录音后，您可以修复需要改进的地方。

让某人参加排练并为您的演讲提供反馈也很有帮助。
我很幸运能与一位导师一起参加这次演讲。 他们给了我关于大纲和幻灯片的反馈，并听取了我的一次排练。

技术准备

除了为我们的演讲找一位导师外，我们还与一位技术检查员配对。 他们在活动开始前一周给了我们一些关于如何处理技术方面的技巧。

那么，你需要准备什么样的技术资料呢？

灯光

这是虚拟谈话中最关键的一项。 你有没有见过带背光的谈话让演讲者的脸看起来很黑？ 或者当他们脸上的光线太亮时？ 或者当演讲者戴上眼镜时，您可以看到他们的光在他们的眼镜上清晰地反射？
与虚拟谈话中的面对面谈话相比，您的听众会更详细地看到您。 整个屏幕只有你（和幻灯片）。

最好的照明是自然光，您可以从阳光中获得。 如果这不可能，您需要自己设置。 然后你可以结合一切来适应你的条件。

相机

你希望你的眼睛在谈话过程中与相机保持一致。 你希望你的听众觉得你在和他们说话。
调整您的坐姿以达到此目的。 如果你没有可调节的椅子，你可以在你的笔记本电脑下面放一些厚书，让它更高，与你的脸保持一致。

幻灯片

除了它的设计之外，我还使用 Canva 来实现它的“演示者视图”功能。 此功能允许您打开两个窗口。 一个是演示者窗口，供您查看幻灯片和脚本，另一个是观众窗口，观众只能看到您的幻灯片。 它还提供时间来跟踪您的通话时间。


请注意，我与 Canva 没有任何关系。 我只喜欢他们的产品。

您需要提前设置并试用您的幻灯片。 您希望在演讲当天准备好幻灯片以进行屏幕共享。 你还需要知道如何共享屏幕——尤其是如果组织者不使用你熟悉的工具——如何控制幻灯片等。

新手虚拟演讲者的额外提示

无需购买额外设备

当然，拥有一个好的相机、麦克风或环形灯会很棒。 但是你可以利用你所拥有的而不是购买。

我借了我丈夫的 720p 网络摄像头，并使用我的游戏耳机和麦克风。 它没有那种低音效果，但它可以提供足够清晰的声音。 我不能利用自然光，因为我的时间是晚上。 于是我拉上了所有窗帘，打开了我丈夫家庭办公室的灯。

至于额外的照明，我在我面前的左右两侧使用了两个台灯。 其中一个没有罩子，所以我的眼镜上有光。 我把它放在我的笔记本电脑支架上以保持光线照射我，但最大限度地减少了我眼镜的反射。

定位您的脚本

这是我的第一次个人演讲。 我很幸运能够虚拟地拥有它。 因为这意味着我可以在我的眼前准备好我的剧本😁。
将您的脚本精确地放置在您的相机下方，并在您想将其阅读到底部时将其向下滚动。 您的观众可以看到您正在阅读，因为您的眼睛会向下滚动。 因此，您希望始终让您的文字尽可能靠近相机。

最小化参与者对 Zoom 的看法

当您召开 Zoom 会议时，会出现一个栏，您可以在其中查看所有参与者。 如果演讲期间有问答，您可能希望看到您的听众。 但是因为在我的情况下没有，我将整个面板最小化，只显示我。 我将它放在我的相机正下方，脚本上方。 它帮助我解决了我的焦虑。 水平定位使我不会分心。 它让我可以在阅读剧本和监控我的肢体语言时将注意力集中在观众（相机）上。

这让我感觉舒服多了，因为我想再排练一次。

最后的话

任何方式的公开演讲都是我最大的氪石。 我对申请成为演讲者从来没有任何信心。 但我了解到，当我们在我们的社区、我们的朋友面前这样做会产生很大的不同，我们知道他们无论如何都会支持我们并让我们振作起来。
我不知道我将来是否会再做一次演讲，但这次演讲对我来说是一次很棒的经历❤。

谢谢你，贝卡，鼓励我！

  • 谢谢你，柯克，你是一位如此伟大的导师！
  • 谢谢你， Suze ，你为我提供了技术方面的重要建议，并给了我一个自信的助推器！
  • 谢谢你，莎拉，在等待轮到我说话时让我保持冷静！
  • 感谢所有 Virtual Coffee 维护者和志愿者使这次谈话成为现实！
  • 谢谢虚拟咖啡朋友们的支持！

    • 感谢您的阅读！
    最后但同样重要的是，您可以在Twitter 上找到我。 让我们连接！ 😊 😊



    原文:

    Hello Fellow Codenewbies 👋,

    Last January, I signed up for CodeNewbie's Challenge 2022: Learn In Public. This five-week challenge taught me how to learn in public through content creation, from creating learning plans to execution.
    They encouraged us to write a series of blog posts, then turn them into audio/visual (A/V) content, such as creating videos or podcasts or submitting a talk.

    Public speaking and A/V content — basically anything that requires me to show my face and talk to the public — are my kryptonite.
    I planned to get out of my comfort zone and try it out. But it didn't happen 🙈.

    Background

    During the CodeNewbie's challenge, Virtual Coffee planned lightning talks. It's a yearly internal members' event where each participating member gives a 10-minute talk about tech-related topics.
    Like in previous years, I didn't have any plan to participate in this talk except to be a cheerleader.

    I do have a few experiences in public speaking. I was a guest in an episode of the Virtual Coffee podcast. Then I participated in a Lunch & Learn at Virtual Coffee to introduce The Collab Lab with two other members as speakers. But I wasn't presenting alone in those events. Solo talk is definitely more challenging for me.

    But something was tickling in me to try this out. The audience of the talk is our members. So I thought, "Hey, it would be okay. It would be like talking to my friends but more formally. If this doesn't work, at least I've tried."

    Well, I did the talk! 😄

    In this article, I want to share how to turn your blog post into a virtual talk based on this experience.
    I hope it can help you if you plan to give your virtual talk for the first time! 😊

    Searching For Topic

    I reached out to Bekah and told her that I had a thought about doing the lightning talk, but I didn't know what topic I could share. I don't feel ready to talk about something technical. She encouraged me to do it and suggested talking about something that I know best and doesn't need to be technical—something around tech that I experienced myself.

    And I realized that what I know best through my experience has to do with being a self-taught developer and technical blogger.

    I've gone through my blog posts and read them one by one.
    And my choice finally goes to one of my articles, 10 Tips For New Self-Taught Developers In Learning To Code. Not only did it come from my own experiences, but later on, this article became very special to me. Someone reached out to me and told me they reread this article to give them strength whenever they feel down. And a few months later, they landed their first job in tech! I'm very thankful to them for letting me know about it. It reminds me of why I write; as a note for myself and to help others.

    The next thing I know, I submitted the form of the lightning talk.

    Turn That Blog Post Into A Talk

    Write the outlines

    Writing outlines is not the same as writing a script. At this point, highlight the things that you want to talk about. Outline the things that are important and remember to stay within the topic. Having bullet points would be helpful.

    Create the slides

    There are many good free slide templates out there. But I use Canva to create mine because it has plenty of lovely designs, even for the free version. Treat slides as a tool to give the audience an idea of what you are talking about.

    Don't write long sentences in your slides. We want the audience to pay attention and get value from what we are sharing and not to distract them by reading the slides.

    Write the script

    After creating the slides, now it's the time to write the script. Write what you want to say for each slide, word by word. Think about storytelling. Try to connect one point to another, one slide to another, and make them flow as one big story.

    Consider the time and how the talk would be

    Would there be a Q&A? If there is one, will the Q&A take place during or after the talk? If it's not clear, you may want to ask the committee about it.

    In my case, each lightning talk speaker got 10 minutes with no Q&A. So, I prepared an 8 minutes talk and gave myself 2 minutes buffer time just in case anything happened.

    Rehearse Time

    You know what? Even people who give talks for many years can feel anxious. And that's the reason why you want to rehearse. Rehearsing doesn't mean memorizing. With rehearsal, you will get used to seeing your slides. Then you get familiar with which slide comes next and what you will say.

    The more you practice, the more you sound natural because you feel much more comfortable.

    It would be helpful to record yourself when you rehearse. I didn't want to do it at first. It would be weird to watch and listen to myself 😅. But finally, I did. I needed to know how I present my talk. I heard from the program committee that they use Zoom for the event. So I use it to practice and record myself.

    I recorded myself 12 times before the talk 😆.

    You can track your timing and listen to your words and intonations by recording yourself. Pay attention to your body language, lighting, and position in front of the camera.
    You can fix what you need to improve after you see the recordings.

    Having someone to join a rehearsal and give you feedback about your talk is also helpful.
    I'm lucky enough to be paired with a mentor for this talk. They gave me feedback on my outlines and slides and listened to one of my rehearsals.

    Technical Preparation

    Besides getting a mentor for our talk, we also got paired with a technical checker. They gave us some tips on how to handle the technical sides of the talk a week before the event.

    So, what kind of technical stuff do you need to prepare?

    Lighting

    It is the most crucial one in a virtual talk. Have you ever seen a talk with a backlight that makes the speaker's face look dark? Or when the light is too bright on their face? Or when the speaker wears glasses, you can see their light reflected clearly on their glasses?
    Your audience will see you in more detail than in-person talk in a virtual talk. The whole screen would be only you (and the slides).

    The best lighting is natural light which you can get from the sunlight. If this is not possible, you need to set it up yourself. There are many good tutorials on YouTube about setting up your light for video conferences. Then you can combine everything to suit your condition.

    Camera

    You want your eyes to be in line with the camera during the talk. You want your audience to feel like you are talking to them.
    Adjust how you sit to reach this. If you don't have an adjustable chair, you can put some thick books under your laptop to make it higher and in line with your face.

    Slides

    Besides its designs, I used Canva for its 'presenter view' feature. This feature allows you to have two windows open. One is the presenter window for you to view your slides and your script, and the other is the audience window where your audience can only see your slides. It also provides the time to track your talk timing.


    Canva's audience window

    Canva's presenter window

    Please be noted that I'm not affiliated with Canva in any way. I only love their products.

    You need to set up and try out your slides in advance. You want to have your slides ready for screen sharing on the day of your talk. You also need to know things like how to share your screen — especially if the organizer doesn't use tools that you're familiar with — how to control your slides, etc.

    Bonus tips from a first-timer virtual speaker

    No need to buy extra equipment

    Of course, it would be great to have a good camera, microphone, or ring light. But you can utilize what you have instead of buying.

    I borrowed my husband's 720p webcam and used my gaming headphone with a microphone. It doesn't have that bass effect, but it does its job of delivering a clear enough sound. I couldn't utilize the natural light because the talk was at night my time. So I closed all curtains and turned on the light in my husband's home office.

    As for the additional lighting, I use two table-side lamps on the right and left sides in front of me. One of them doesn't have a cover, so the light flare on my glasses. I put it on my laptop stand to keep the light on me but minimized my glasses' reflection.

    Position your script

    It was my first solo talk. And I was lucky to have it virtually. Because it means that I could have my script ready in front of my eyes 😁.
    Position your script precisely under your camera and scroll it down whenever you feel like you will read it to the bottom. Your audience can see you're reading because your eyes will scroll down. So you want to keep your text as close as possible with the camera all the time.

    Minimize the participants' view on Zoom

    When you have a Zoom meeting, there is a bar where you can see all participants. You might want to see your audience if there is a Q&A during the talk. But because there was none in my case, I minimized the whole panel to show only me. I positioned it right under my camera, above the script. It helped me address my anxiety. The horizontal positioning kept me from getting distracted. It gave me control to focus my eyes on the audience (camera) while reading my script and monitoring my body language.

    And it made me feel much more comfortable because I felt like having another rehearsal.

    Final Words

    Public speaking in any way is my biggest kryptonite. I never had any confidence in applying to be a speaker. But I learned that it made a big difference when we do it in front of our community, our friends, whom we know would support and cheer us up no matter what.
    I don't know if I will ever give another talk in the future, but this talk was one great experience for me ❤.

    🙏 Thank you notes (in no particular order):

    • Thank you, Bekah, for encouraging me!
    • Thank you, Kirk, for being such a great mentor!
    • Thank you, Suze, for providing me with great tips on technical aspects and for giving me a confident booster!
    • Thank you, Sara, for keeping me calm while waiting for my turn to talk!
    • Thank you to all Virtual Coffee maintainers and volunteers for making this talk happen!
    • Thank you, Virtual Coffee friends, for all your support!

    Thank you for reading!
    Last but not least, you can find me on Twitter. Let's connect! 😊
item.author: adiatiayu

item.title: 重温痛苦：没有 Git 的世界 | Revisiting the Pain: The World without Git
item.description:
计算机在很短的时间内发展了很多，并且仍在迅速发展。 随着技术的出现，世界现在就在我们的指尖。 随着硬件领域的这些进步，软件开发也取得了很大进展。 当我们谈论软件开发时，怎么能忘记Git呢？

如果您是软件开发人员，那么它就是您的生计。 但是 Git 是在2005年开发的（引发了激烈的争议），现在软件的开发已经有 7 多年的历史了。 那么，在 Git 之前，开发世界是怎样的。

让我们深入了解它。

在软件开发的早期阶段，不存在版本控制系统。 每日或每周备份是常态。

对于协作项目，过去通常有一个主副本，通常由一个人控制。 每个人都和那个人一起工作。
在那些日子里， Commit意味着备份当前版本并应用新的更改。

这带来了两个主要问题：

除了手动检查文件的差异之外，别无选择。

没有 VCS 的所有任务都很乏味。 这需要发布团队和项目负责人付出更多的努力。 为了解决这个问题，程序员开发了Local VCS 。 它们也被称为“第一代 VCS” 。

顾名思义，它有一个本地数据库来存储对文件所做的更改。 最流行的本地 VCS 之一是“ RCS（修订控制系统） ”，它存储补丁集并可以通过合并这些补丁集来重现文件在任何时间点的外观。

另一个流行的第一代本地 VCS 是“ SCCS ”或源代码控制系统，它确实是 UNIX 开发人员在 1972 年开发的第一个 VCS。

本地 VCS 比维护备份好或至少更好，但即使它也有问题。 一切都存储locally 。 如果system crashed并且没有备份，您将失去一切。 此外， no way to collaborate与其他开发人员合作。

为了解决这个问题，开发了集中式 VCS。 他们是“第二代 VCS”

在这里，我们有一个中央服务器，其中包含对项目所做的所有版本和提交。 每个人都可以推动和拉动这些变化。 由于服务器是集中式的，几乎每个人都知道对方在做什么。

除了所有这些优点之外，CVCS 还提出了缺点：

您可能会从提交最后一次更改但所有先前更改都消失的开发人员那里获得项目的最新版本。

所以，如果你在没有互联网的地方工作，你可能会过得很糟糕。

第二代 VCS 的一些示例是“ Apache Subversion(SVN) ”、“ CVS ”和“ Perforce ”。

早先的一切都有它的优点和缺点。 但是需要更好的东西，这就是分布式 VCS 出现的时候。 它也被称为“第三代 VCS” 。

在这里，每个人都locally on their machine和centralized server上对项目进行了完整备份。 由于每个人都拥有整个工作历史的本地副本，因此无需在线即可提交更改。 他们可以先将更改提交到本地存储库，只要有互联网可用，他们就可以将这些更改推送到主存储库（远程存储库）。

第三代 VCS 的一些示例是"Git" 、 "Mercurial" 、 "Bitkeeper"和"bzr" 。

直到2002 年，更改都是作为补丁和存档文件传递的。 对于查看项目范围的开发人员来说，这非常困难。

所以，终于在2002 年，他们开始使用当时免费使用的“Bitkeeper VCS” 。 一切都很好，Linux 开发进展顺利，直到2005 年，Bitkeeper 的版权所有者Larry McVoy在声称Andrew Tridgell通过逆向工程Bitkeeper 的协议创建了Sourcepuller后撤销了free-of-charge状态。

正是在这个时候，Linux 的创造者Linus Torvalds考虑开发自己的分布式 VCS 并提供他们需要的功能。 这导致了在软件开发领域中最常用的 VCS 的开发。

学分

图片：专业 Git

参考

专业 Git 电子书： https ://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
维基百科： https ://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Git



原文:

Computers have evolved a lot in a short span of time and are still evolving rapidly. With the advent of technology, the world is now at our finger tips. Along with these advancements in the hardware sector, software development has also progressed a lot. And when we talk about software development, how can one forget Git?

Git is the single most-used version control system(VCS). Git is superfast and it seems as if Gods of speed have blessed Git with unworldly powers. If you're a Software developer, it's your bread and butter. But Git was developed in 2005(with a blazing controversy) and software is being developed for more than 7 decades now. So, how did the development world look before Git.

Let's dive right into it.

1) Maintaining Backups locally

In earlier stages of software development, there was no existence of a version controlling system. Daily or Weekly backups was the norm.

For a collaborative project, there used to be a master copy usually under a single person's control. Everyone worked with that person.
Back in those days, a Commit meant backing up the current version and applying the new changes.

This came up with 2 major problems :

1) It was hard to know what changes you made over time. There was no other option than manually checking the files for differences.

2) Overtime, the files would become so large that making multiple of them may eventually take up your whole disk storage.

2) Local VCS (LVCS)

All the tasks without VCS were tedious. It required more effort from release team and the project leaders. To solve this, programmers developed Local VCS. They are also referred to as "First Generation VCS".

As the name suggests, it had a local database to store the changes made to the files. One of the most popular local VCS was "RCS(Revision Control System)" which stored patch sets and could reproduce how the file looked at any point of time by merging those patch sets.

Another popular first generation local VCS was "SCCS" or Source Code Control System which was indeed the first VCS developed in 1972 by UNIX developers.

3) Centralized VCS (CVCS)

Local VCS were good or at least better than maintaining backups but even it had a problem. Everything was stored locally. Incase if the system crashed and there's no backup you would lose everything. Also, there was no way to collaborate with other developers.

To solve this issue, Centralized VCS was developed. They were the "Second Generation VCS"

Here, we had a central server which contained all the versions and commits ever made to the project. Everyone could push and pull the changes. As the server was centralized, almost everyone knew what the other person was working upon.

Along with all these pros, CVCS also came up with cons:

1) As everything was centralized, if there's a server failure or the hard disk of the server gets corrupted, you lose everything(if there are no backups). You may get the latest version of the project from some developer who committed the last change but all the previous changes are gone.

2) Also, due to centralized server, you need to have an Internet connection if you want to commit your changes. So, if you're working in a place with no internet, you may have a bad time.

Some of the examples of Second Generation VCS are "Apache Subversion(SVN)", "CVS" and "Perforce".

4) Distributed VCS (DVCS)

Everything earlier came with it's pros and cons. But there was a need of something even better and that's when Distributed VCS came into picture. It was also referred to as the "Third Generation VCS".

Here, everyone had the full backup of the project both locally on their machine and also on the centralized server. As everyone had a local copy of entire work history, one doesn't need to be online to commit their changes. They can commit their changes to local repository first and whenever there's an availability of internet, they can push those changes to the master repository(remote repository).

Some of the examples of Third Generation VCS are "Git", "Mercurial", "Bitkeeper" and "bzr".

A Short history of GIT

Linux development started in the year 1991. Until the year 2002, changes were passed as patches and archived files. This was very difficult for the developers looking at the scope of the project.

So, finally in the year 2002, they began using "Bitkeeper VCS" which was free-to-use at that time. Everything was good and the Linux development was going smoothly up until 2005 when Bitkeeper's copyright holder Larry McVoy revoked the free-of-charge status after claiming that Andrew Tridgell created Sourcepuller by reverse engineering Bitkeeper's protocols.

This was the time when Linus Torvalds, the creator of Linux, thought of developing their own Distributed VCS with the features they needed. The development of Git began on 3 April, 2005 and achieved it's performance goals on 29 April, 2005.

This led to the development of the single-most used VCS in the software development world.

Credits

Images: Pro Git

References

Pro Git eBook: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Git
item.author: sneh27

item.title: Fula：一种开发去中心化应用程序的创新方式 | Fula: a new, innovative way to develop decentralized applications
item.description:
问候开发人员！ 首先，衷心感谢 DEV.TO 社区对 Functionland 的所有支持！

作为开发团队中一个相对较新的成员，我可以告诉你， @ben对我们之前帖子的评论是我发现这个项目时首先阅读的内容之一。

从那以后很多事情改变了。 是时候更新了！ 希望我们现在可以在众筹活动的最后几天获得您的更多支持。

在我开始更新之前，我想分享一下第一次引起我对Fula协议的共鸣； 我们在上次DEV.TO 更新中提到的开源 p2p 协议。

在加入 Functionland 之前，我想了解更多关于 web3 的信息。 所以我加入了一个eth 全球黑客马拉松。 在那里，我在一个令牌门控实时视频流媒体网站上工作，创作者可以用它来举办在线售票音乐会。

当我得知 web3 中访问控制的现状仍然倾向于中心化，依靠单个节点来验证 NFT 持有者与他们的钱包地址匹配时，我有点失望。

我感到很失望。 编写联网的无许可软件的吸引力消失了。 我发现我必须成为我的“DApp”用户的桥梁守护者。

这意味着我必须建立自己的云帐户并监控使用情况，并确保如果使用量激增，我有办法支付账单。

这也意味着我不能再声称没有许可了，因为我随时都可以拔掉插头。

我陷入了一个兔子洞，寻找更好的去中心化解决方案，让我 - 第三方 DApp 开发人员 - 能够摆脱困境！ 毕竟，这不是 web3 的大承诺吗？ 总是在无许可计算上？

我研究了很多去中心化计算项目，得出了两个结论：

  1. 在我自己的计算中使用代理时，我认为不可能完全消除“信任”。

  2. 没有“自由能”这样的东西。 如果有人提供“免费云计算”，那一定是个问题。

然后我发现了 Functionland。 让我产生共鸣的是，团队正在为人们构建一个解决方案（Box），用新的东西代替云计算，而不仅仅是另一个原生云解决方案。

使用定制的即插即用硬件为消费者提供计算，这样他们就不必在便利性上妥协。

该解决方案继续提供消费者认为理所当然的云属性，例如可用性和可靠性。 它还为消费者提供了云单独无法提供的新元素：

  • 真正无需许可的 DApp。 开发人员不必持有任何 API 密钥

  • 额外的安全和隐私。 用户可以选择在自己的硬件上运行一切

  • 完整的数据所有权。 我上传的所有内容也都位于我自己的家庭服务器上

让我们花点时间（深吸一口气），欣赏为消费者提供混合解决方案的重要性。 在过去的几十年里，企业难道没有将所有数据从自己的服务器迁移到云数据中心，以降低配备自己系统管理员的成本吗？

是的，但是任何高薪系统管理员都可以告诉您，许多组织没有也不会将所有数据完全迁移到云中。 为什么？ 因为某些数据（又名记录系统）对业务非常重要，因此不值得信任其他人。

那么，为什么消费者需要只有最大的企业才能拥有的东西呢？ 为了回答这个问题，让我们做一个小小的思想实验。 假设我们从系统设计的角度提出了与个人云存储提供商基本相同的解决方案，除了我们使用分散的存储网络。

这很好，因为我们现在让任何人（具有技术知识）成为存储提供商。 但是当您停止支付账单并且您的设备关闭时会发生什么？ 您必须致电每个存储提供商的客户支持，并要求他们真诚地保留您的数据，即使您拖欠账单。 这对我来说听起来不对。 尤其是当我们谈论我的个人家庭照片和视频时——我希望有一天能传给我的孩子的大部分遗产。

因此，在翻了个白眼并对自己说“不是另一个 web3 协议”之后，我开始明白需要一些新的东西，它不仅可以利用对等网络的前沿优势，而且承认客户端-服务器架构也不会消失（这在一个充满低功耗嵌入式设备的世界中非常重要）。

现在......让我们回到那个更新！

开发团队一直在努力开发一个可用的Fotos DApp 、 Fula （我一直在抱怨的 web3 协议）和 Box（它的参考实现）。

我们还为一个新的综合文档站点投入了大量时间和考虑，感兴趣的外部人员可以访问该站点：

  • 学习如何在 Fula 之上开发自己的富 web3 DApps

  • 阅读我们最新的白皮书

  • 在RFC 部分查看我们为未来工作而设计的最新设计

但是，它的设计目的不仅仅是为了备份和分享来自 react 原生移动客户端的照片。

我们还提供了 Todo、Web Gallery 和 Doc 同步示例 DApp，以说明如何为 web3 开发 DApp 比为 web2 开发应用程序更容易！

最后，文档同步配套 DApp说明了以下内容：

  • 如何使用 Graph API 实时订阅和接收事件

  • 除了照片之外，还可以处理不同的文件格式，例如纯文本降价

  • 如何通过拦截底层操作系统提供的文件系统更改事件并通过调用文件 API 对这些更改采取行动，从而实时共享文件更新

  • 开发人员如何添加一个简单的部署脚本，该脚本在应用程序的构建输出上调用IPFS add -r作为 package.json 部署脚本（无需云托管帐户）

要了解如何运行示例以及如何使用 FULA 客户端库，开发人员可以前往 docs.fx.land 上的入门部分。

最近，除了改进 Graph 和 File API 之外，该团队还通过重构 Box 架构以包括IPFS Cluster来提高本地池的 DApp 可用性和可靠性。

我们还通过使用分散标识符 (DID) 支持客户端加密。

这就是更新！ 我们要感谢我们试图在短时间内克服的技术山峰。 十多年来，有很多非常聪明的人一直在努力解决我们试图解决的问题。

我们相信我们可以在相对较短的时间内到达那里，原因如下：

  • 激光专注于在我们发货时交付与旗舰 DApp 相关的内容

  • 得益于开源开发社区的支持（就是您！）

  • 最重要的是站在巨人的肩膀上（从硬件到软件）

谢谢，我们非常期待收到来自精彩的 DEV.TO 社区关于我们正在构建的内容的反馈！

一如既往，请随时抨击我们的想法！ 我们仍在学习，我们认为你们都是狂热的。 当有东西卡在牙齿上时，弗伦茨会告诉弗伦茨。 :D :D



原文:

Greetings fellow DEVs! First off, a warm thank you to the DEV.TO community for all the support they’ve given Functionland!

As a relatively new member of the dev team, I can tell you that @ben's comment on our previous post was one of the first things I read when I discovered this project.

A lot has changed since then. Time for an update! Hopefully we can garner EVEN MORE support from you now, in the final days of our crowdfunding campaign.

Before I get to the updates I'd like to share what first resonated with me about the Fula protocol; the open source p2p protocol we mentioned in the last DEV.TO update.

Before joining Functionland I wanted to learn more about web3. So I joined an eth global hackathon. There I worked on a token gated livepeer video streaming site that creators could use to host an online ticketed concert.

I was a little disappointed when I learned that the status quo for access control in web3 still tended to be centralised, relying on a single node to verify that the NFT holder matches their wallet address.

I felt let down. The appeal of writing networked permissionless software vanished. I’d discovered I had to be a bridge keeper to users of my “DApp”.

This meant I'd have to set up my own cloud account and monitor usage and make sure that if usage spikes I have a way to pay the bills.

It also meant that I couldn’t really claim permissionless anymore because at any moment I could pull the plug.

I went down a bit of a rabbit hole looking for better decentralized solutions that would enable me - a third party DApp developer - to get out of the way! After all, isn’t that the big promise of web3? Always on permissionless computing?

I looked into a lot of decentralized computing projects and came to two conclusions:

  1. When it comes to using a surrogate for my own computations, I don't think it’s possible to completely remove 'trust'.

  2. There is no such thing as 'free energy.' If someone is offering 'free cloud computing' there must be a catch.

Then I discovered Functionland. What resonated with me was the fact that the team was building a solution (Box) for people to replace cloud computing with something new, not just another native cloud solution.

The Functionland team was proposing a completely new paradigm - hybrid cloud/on prem. computing for consumers with customized plug and play hardware so that they don't have to compromise on convenience.

This solution continues to offer the properties of cloud that consumers take for granted, like availability and reliability. It also offers consumers new elements that cloud alone can’t provide:

  • truly permissionless DApps. The developer doesn’t have to hold any API keys

  • additional security and privacy. The user can choose to run everything on their own hardware

  • complete data ownership. Everything I upload also sits on my own home server

Let's take a moment (and a deep breath) and appreciate the magnitude of offering a hybrid soolution for consumers. Hasn't enterprise spent the last few decades migrating all of their data from their own servers to a cloud data centre to cut down on the cost of staffing their own sys admins?

Yes but any high paid sys admin out there can tell you many organisations have not and will not ever fully migrate all their data to the cloud. Why? Because there is certain data (aka systems of record) that is so important to the business that it isn’t worthwhile to trust someone else with it.

So why do consumers need what only the largest enterprises have? To answer that, let’s do a little thought experiment. Let’s say we came up with essentially the same solution from a system design perspective as the personal cloud storage providers, except we use a decentralized storage network.

That’s great because we are now enabling anyone (with the technical know how) to be a storage provider. But what happens when you stop paying the bills and your device turns off? You would have to call each individual storage provider's customer support and ask them to please hold on to your data in good faith even though you are behind on a bill. That doesn’t sound right to me. Especially since we are talking about my personal home photos and video - a big part of my legacy that I would like to some day pass down to my children.

So, after rolling my eyes and saying to myself 'not another web3 protocol', I began to understand the need for something new that not only takes advantage of the cutting edge in peer to peer networking but also acknowledges the fact that the client-server architecture is also not going away (which is incredibly important in a world full of low-powered, embedded devices).

Now… Let’s get back to that update!

The dev team has been hard at work developing a usable Fotos DApp, Fula (the web3 protocol I've been ranting about) and Box (it's reference implementation).

We've also put a lot of time and consideration into a new consolidated docs site where interested externals can come to:

  • learn how to develop their own rich web3 DApps on top of Fula

  • read our latest whitepaper

  • look at our latest designs for future work in the RFCs section

The Fula protocol was designed to power the Fotos DApp as a Web3 decentralised application using open interoperable specifications that include IPFS and libp2p.

However, it was not designed only for the purpose of backing up and sharing photos from a react native mobile client.

We also provide Todo, Web Gallery and Doc syncing sample DApps to illustrate how developing DApps for web3 can be even easier than developing apps for web2!

The TODOs sample DApp illustrates making CRUD operations over graphql and the gallery sample DApp illustrates how to use the FILE and GRAPH APIs in conjunction with each other.

Finally, the doc syncing companion DApps illustrate the following:

  • how one can subscribe to and receive events in realtime using the Graph API

  • working with different file formats besides photos such as plain text markdown

  • how updates to files can be shared in real-time by intercepting file system change events provided by the underlying OS and acting on those changes by invoking the File API

  • how a developer could add a simple deploy script that calls IPFS add -r on the app's build output as a package.json deploy script (no cloud hosting account needed)

To learn how to get the samples running and how to work with the FULA client libraries, developers can head over to the getting started section on docs.fx.land.

Recently, besides refining the Graph and File APIs, the team has made some first steps towards improving DApp availability and reliability with local pools by refactoring the Box architecture to include IPFS Cluster.

We have also included support for client side encryption through the use of decentralised identifiers (DIDs).

That’s it for the update! We would like to acknowledge the technical mountain we are trying to overcome in a short amount of time. There are incredibly smart people that have been working on the problem we are trying to solve for over a decade.

We believe we can get there in a relatively short amount of time because of the following:

  • laser focus on delivering what matters with the flagship DApps when we ship

  • exceptional funding thanks to support from the open source development community (that is YOU!)

  • most importantly, standing on the shoulders of giants (from hardware to software)

Thanks and we really look forward to hearing feedback from the wonderful DEV.TO community about what we are building!

As always, please feel free to bash our idea! We are still learning and we consider you all as frenz. Frenz tell frenz when they have something stuck in their teeth. :D
item.author: gitaaron

item.title: React Tricks Miniseries 4：如何更有效地传递道具 | React Tricks Miniseries 4: How to pass props more efficiently
item.description:
将 props 传递给子组件可能很简单，但许多开发人员都做错了。 不过我们都去过那里。 大多数使用传递道具以两种方式之一。 要么传递我们希望使用的每个属性，要么传递一个包含我们要使用的道具的对象。

方法一

< Card cardObject = { cardObject } / >

方法二

< Card title = { title } description = { description } rating = { rating } / >

在第一种情况下，我们需要以一种效率较低的方式检索 Card 组件中的 props：

 export default function Card ( props ) { const { title , description , rating } = props . cardObject }

而在第二种情况下，当道具列表变长时，将它们一个一个添加起来变得太低效了。

最佳实践方法是像这样使用对象解构：

 < Card {... cardObject } / > //then retrieve the props in the usualy way export default function Card ({ title , description , rating }) { }

这种方法更清洁、更高效，并且对于长列表道具的扩展性更好。

结论

使用对象解构，我们可以实现更好更高效的 p​​rops 传递给 react 组件。

你一直用这个方法吗？ 还是现在才学的？ 在评论中告诉我！



原文:

React is all about props and the more you use reusable components, the more scaleable your react app will become (if done correctly, of course).

Passing props to a child component may be trivial, but many developers do it wrong. We've all been there though. Most of use pass props in one of two way. Either passing every property we wish to use, or passing an object containing the props we want to use.

Method 1 

<Card cardObject={cardObject}
/>

Method 2 

<Card title={title} description={description} rating={rating}
/>

With the first scenario, we'd need to retreive the props inside the Card component, in a less than efficient way:

export default function Card(props) { const { title, description, rating } = props.cardObject
}

And with the second scenario, when the list of props gets long, it becomes too inefficient to add them all one by one.

The best practice method is to use object destructuring like this:

<Card {...cardObject}
/>
//then retrieve the props in the usualy way
export default function Card({ title, description, rating }) { }

This method is a lot cleaner, more efficient and scales much better for long lists of props.

Conclusion

Using object destructuring, we can achieve better and more efficient props passing to react components.

Have you always used this method? Or just learned it now? Let me know in the comments!
item.author: theeasydev

item.title: 如何使用 NVM 解决 QueryCursor.js Mongoose 错误 | How to Solve QueryCursor.js Mongoose error with NVM
item.description:
这是我尝试解决在GitHub 上提出的这个问题。 我发现这是一个 NodeJS 不兼容问题。 将您的NodeJS版本降级到12.x可以解决此错误。

如果您使用的是 Mongoose 版本 6.3.2 并且您的 NodeJS 版本已更新到 >14.x 或 =17.5.0（这是我撰写本文时的最新版本），您可能会遇到此错误：

 TypeError: Cannot assign to read only property 'map' of object '#<QueryCursor>' at Object.<anonymous> (/Users/balonsom/Documents/repos/dungeon-master/node_modules/mongoose/lib/cursor/QueryCursor.js:144:27) at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1097:14) at Object.Module._extensions..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1151:10) at Module.load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:975:32) at Function.Module._load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:822:12) at Module.require (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:999:19) at require (node:internal/modules/cjs/helpers:102:18) at Object.<anonymous> (/Users/balonsom/Documents/repos/dungeon-master/node_modules/mongoose/lib/query.js:12:21) at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1097:14) at Object.Module._extensions..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1151:10)

上述错误是由 Mongoose 和 NodeJS 17.5 版本之间的不兼容问题引起的。 在引发问题 #11377的 Mongoose GitHub存储库中，提供了一些建议，我尝试了所有但没有一个为我解决错误。 此外，问题#11377已被问题#11381标记为已解决，而我的情况并非如此。

如何解决 QueryCursor.js Mongoose 错误

知道这是 NodeJS 和 Mongoose 之间的兼容性问题，最好的解决方案是将我的 Node.js 版本降级到版本 14 之前的版本🤔

但是等等，我在本地机器上运行的其他项目与 <16.x 的 Node.js 版本不兼容😟

这就是NVM -（节点版本管理器）发挥作用的地方。 有关如何安装和使用 NVM 的深入指南，请参阅 Digital Ocean 的本教程。

安装和使用 NVM

在本指南中，我将引导您了解安装 NVM 和在您的 Mongoose 项目中使用 Node.js v12 的基本知识。

如何安装 NVM

要安装 NVM，请运行以下命令

curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.34.0/install.sh | bash

或使用wget

 wget -qO- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.34.0/install.sh | bash

安装后，关闭终端应用程序以使更改生效。 您还需要在 bash shell 启动文件中添加几行。 此文件的名称可能为.bashrc 、 .bash_profile或.zshrc ，具体取决于您的操作系统。 为此，请重新打开您的终端应用程序并运行以下命令：

 export NVM_DIR = " $HOME /.nvm" [ -s " $NVM_DIR /nvm.sh" ] && \. " $NVM_DIR /nvm.sh" [ -s " $NVM_DIR /bash_completion" ] && \. " $NVM_DIR /bash_completion"

安装 NodeJS v12.x

要安装 NodeJS 12，请将以下命令粘贴到您的终端中

运行此命令后，这是将显示在终端应用程序中的输出：

 Output Downloading and installing node v12.22.12... Downloading https://nodejs.org/dist/v12.22.12/node-v12.22.12-darwin-x64.tar.xz... ######################################################################### 100.0% Computing checksum with shasum -a 256 Checksums matched! Now using node v12.22.12 (npm v6.14.16)

您已经在本地机器上成功安装了 NodeJS v12。 这意味着您现在正在运行多个版本的 NodeJS。

列出您机器上已安装的 Node.js 版本

安装了几个不同版本的 Node.js 后，我们可以使用ls参数运行nvm来列出我们已安装的所有内容：

运行此命令产生的输出可能如下所示：

 v12.22.12 v14.19.2 v17.5.0 system default -> node ( -> v17.5.0 ) iojs -> N/A ( default ) unstable -> N/A ( default ) node -> stable ( -> v17.5.0 ) ( default ) stable -> 17.5 ( -> v17.5.0 ) ( default ) lts/ * -> lts/gallium ( -> N/A ) lts/argon -> v4.9.1 ( -> N/A ) lts/boron -> v6.17.1 ( -> N/A ) lts/carbon -> v8.17.0 ( -> N/A ) lts/dubnium -> v10.24.1 ( -> N/A ) lts/erbium -> v12.22.12 lts/fermium -> v14.19.2 lts/gallium -> v16.15.0 ( -> N/A )

您的输出肯定会与我的不同，具体取决于您在机器上安装了多少个 Node.js 版本。

下一步需要我们指定要在程序或项目中使用的 NodeJS 版本。 为此，请按照以下步骤操作：

在我们的项目中使用 NodeJS v12

通过运行此命令cd进入您的项目目录

cd <path/to/your/project/directory>

然后在下面运行这个命令来指定要使用的 NodeJS 版本，在我们的例子中是 v12

您将获得以下输出

Now using node v12.22.12 (npm v6.14.16)

恭喜🎆🎇🎊🎈

您现在正在为这个特定项目使用 NodeJS 版本，同时还在您的机器上保留 Node.js 的默认活动版本。

进一步阅读

跟我来

  1. 在推特上@eunit99
  2. 在 GitHub @eunit99


原文:

TL;DR

This is my attempt at resolving this issue raised on GitHub. I discovered that it was a NodeJS incompatibility issue. Downgrading your NodeJS version to 12.x resolves this error.

If you are working with Mongoose version 6.3.2 and you had your NodeJS version updated to >14.x or =17.5.0 (this is the latest version as of when I am writing this post), you might have encountered this error:

TypeError: Cannot assign to read only property 'map' of object '#<QueryCursor>'
    at Object.<anonymous> (/Users/balonsom/Documents/repos/dungeon-master/node_modules/mongoose/lib/cursor/QueryCursor.js:144:27)
    at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1097:14)
    at Object.Module._extensions..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1151:10)
    at Module.load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:975:32)
    at Function.Module._load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:822:12)
    at Module.require (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:999:19)
    at require (node:internal/modules/cjs/helpers:102:18)
    at Object.<anonymous> (/Users/balonsom/Documents/repos/dungeon-master/node_modules/mongoose/lib/query.js:12:21)
    at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1097:14)
    at Object.Module._extensions..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1151:10)

The above error is caused by an incompatibility issue between Mongoose and NodeJS version 17.5. On the Mongoose GitHub repo where the issue #11377 was raised, several suggestions were offered which I tried all but none resolved the error for me. Also, issue #11377 was marked resolved by issue #11381 which was not the case for me.

How to Solve QueryCursor.js Mongoose error

Knowing that this is a compatibility issue between NodeJS and Mongoose, the best solution will be to downgrade my Node.js version to something earlier than version 14 🤔

But wait, I have other projects on my local machine that I am running which are incompatible with Node.js versions <16.x 😟

This is where NVM - (Node Version Manager) comes into play. NVM allows us to install and run multiple versions of NodeJS on our local machine. For an in-depth guide on how to install and use NVM, please refer to this tutorial by Digital Ocean.

Installing and Using NVM

In this guide, I shall walk you through the essentials of installing NVM and using Node.js v12 for your Mongoose project.

How to Install NVM

To install NVM, run the following commands

curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.34.0/install.sh | bash

or using wget

wget -qO- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.34.0/install.sh | bash

Once installed, close your terminal application for changes to take effect. You will also need to add a couple of lines to your bash shell startup file. This file might have the name .bashrc, .bash_profile, or .zshrc depending on your operating system. To do this, reopen your terminal app and run the following commands:

export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && \. "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion" ] && \. "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion"

Installing NodeJS v12.x

To install NodeJS 12, paste the following command into your terminal

After running this command, this is the output that will display in your terminal app:

Output
Downloading and installing node v12.22.12...
Downloading https://nodejs.org/dist/v12.22.12/node-v12.22.12-darwin-x64.tar.xz...
######################################################################### 100.0%
Computing checksum with shasum -a 256
Checksums matched!
Now using node v12.22.12 (npm v6.14.16)

You have successfully installed NodeJS v12 on your local machine. This means that you are now running multiple versions of NodeJS.

List installed Node.js versions on your machine

With a handful of different versions of Node.js installed, we can run nvm with the ls argument to list out everything we have installed:

The output produced by running this command might look something like this:

v12.22.12 v14.19.2 v17.5.0 system
default -> node (-> v17.5.0)
iojs -> N/A (default)
unstable -> N/A (default)
node -> stable (-> v17.5.0) (default)
stable -> 17.5 (-> v17.5.0) (default)
lts/* -> lts/gallium (-> N/A)
lts/argon -> v4.9.1 (-> N/A)
lts/boron -> v6.17.1 (-> N/A)
lts/carbon -> v8.17.0 (-> N/A)
lts/dubnium -> v10.24.1 (-> N/A)
lts/erbium -> v12.22.12
lts/fermium -> v14.19.2
lts/gallium -> v16.15.0 (-> N/A)

Your output will definitely differ from mine depending on how many versions of Node.js you have installed on your machine.

The next step entails us specifying which version of NodeJS we want to use in our program or project. To do this, follow the below steps:

Using NodeJS v12 in our project

cd into the directory of your project by running this command

cd <path/to/your/project/directory>

Then run this command below to specify the NodeJS version want to use, in our case, v12

You will get the following output

Now using node v12.22.12 (npm v6.14.16)

Congratulations 🎆🎇🎊🎈

You are now using NodeJS version for this specific project, while also preserving the default active version of Node.js on your machine.

Further reading

Follow Me

  1. On Twitter @eunit99
  2. On GitHub @eunit99
item.author: eunit

item.title: 了解 JavaScript 中的闭包 | Understanding closures in JavaScript
item.description:
闭包是可以从另一个函数的作用域访问变量的函数。 这通常通过在函数内部创建函数来完成。

 function createComparisonFunction(propertyName) { return function(object1, object2) { let value1 = object1[propertyName]; let value2 = object2[propertyName]; if (value1 < value2) { return -1; } else if (value1> value2) { return 1; } else { return 0; } }; }

此示例中突出显示的行是从外部函数访问变量 (propertyName) 的内部函数（匿名函数）的一部分。 即使内部函数已返回并在其他地方使用，它也可以访问该变量。 这是因为内部函数的作用域链包括 createComparisonFunction() 的作用域。

函数的激活对象使用参数和任何命名参数的值进行初始化。 外部函数的激活对象是作用域链中的第二个对象。 对于所有包含函数，此过程将继续，直到作用域链以全局执行上下文终止。

在函数执行时，会在作用域链中查找变量以读取和写入值。

 function compare(value1, value2) { if (value1 < value2) { return -1; } else if (value1> value2) { return 1; } else { return 0; } } let result = compare(5, 10);

此代码定义了一个名为 compare() 的函数，该函数在全局执行上下文中调用。 当第一次调用 compare() 时，会创建一个包含参数、value1 和 value2 的新激活对象。 全局执行上下文的变量对象位于 compare() 执行上下文的作用域链中，其中包含 this、result 和 compare。

这个对象

在闭包中使用 this 对象会引入一些复杂的行为。 当函数未使用箭头语法定义时，this 对象在运行时根据函数执行的上下文绑定：当在全局函数中使用时，this 等于非严格模式下的 window 和严格模式下的 undefined，而当作为对象方法调用时，this 等于对象。

在此上下文中，匿名函数未绑定到对象，这意味着除非在严格模式下执行，否则 this 对象指向窗口。 然而，由于闭包的编写方式，这一事实并不总是显而易见的。

 window.identity = 'The Window'; let object = { identity: 'My Object', getIdentityFunc() { return function() { return this.identity; }; } }; console.log(object.getIdentityFunc()()); // 'The Window'

请记住，每个函数在调用函数时都会自动获取两个特殊变量：this 和 arguments。 内部函数永远不能直接从外部函数访问这些变量。 可以通过将闭包存储在闭包可以访问的另一个变量中来允许闭包访问不同的 this 对象。

 window.identity = 'The Window'; let object = { identity: 'My Object', getIdentityFunc() { let that = this; return function() { return that.identity; }; } }; console.log(object.getIdentityFunc()()); // 'My Object'

您不太可能故意使用第二行或第三行中的模式，但是知道当语法稍有变化时 this 的值可能会以意想不到的方式发生变化是有帮助的。

此链接了解有关关闭的更多信息

支持我获取更多内容



原文:

Closures are functions that have access to variables from another function's scope. This is often accomplished by creating a function inside a function.

function createComparisonFunction(propertyName) {         
 return function(object1, object2) {
  let value1 = object1[propertyName];
  let value2 = object2[propertyName];

  if (value1 < value2) {
   return -1;
  } else if (value1> value2) {
   return 1;
  } else {
   return 0;
  }
 };
}

The highlighted lines in this example are part of the inner function (an anonymous function) that is accessing a variable (propertyName) from the outer function. Even after the inner function has been returned and is being used elsewhere, it has access to that variable. This occurs because the inner function's scope chain includes the scope of createComparisonFunction().

The activation object for the function is initialized with values for arguments and any named arguments. The outer function's activation object is the second object in the scope chain. This process continues for all containing functions until the scope chain terminates with the global execution context.

As the function executes, variables are looked up in the scope chain for the reading and writing of values.

function compare(value1, value2) {
 if (value1 < value2) {
  return -1;
 } else if (value1> value2) {
  return 1;
 } else {
  return 0;
 }
}

let result = compare(5, 10);

This code defines a function named compare() that is called in the global execution context. When compare() is called for the first time, a new activation object is created that contains arguments, value1, and value2. The global execution context's variable object is next in the compare() execution context's scope chain, which contains this, result, and compare.

The this Object

Using the this object inside closures introduces some complex behaviors. When a function is not defined using the arrow syntax, the this object is bound at runtime based on the context in which a function is executed: when used inside global functions, this is equal to window in nonstrict mode and undefined in strict mode, whereas this is equal to the object when called as an object method.

Anonymous functions are not bound to an object in this context, meaning the this object points to window unless executing in strict mode. Because of the way closures are written, however, this fact is not always obvious.

window.identity = 'The Window';

let object = {
 identity: 'My Object',      
 getIdentityFunc() {
  return function() {
   return this.identity;
  };
 }
};

console.log(object.getIdentityFunc()()); // 'The Window'

Remember that each function automatically gets two special variables as soon as the function is called: this and arguments. An inner function can never access these variables directly from an outer function. It is possible to allow a closure access to a different this object by storing it in another variable that the closure can access.

window.identity = 'The Window';

let object = {
 identity: 'My Object',      
 getIdentityFunc() {
  let that = this;
  return function() {
   return that.identity;
  };
 }
};

console.log(object.getIdentityFunc()()); // 'My Object'

It's unlikely that you'll intentionally use the patterns in lines two or three, but it is helpful to know that the value of this can change in unexpected ways when syntax is changed slightly.

this link for more information about closures

Ismlar, harflar, raqamlar va unc bolishi mumkin ismlar harf yoki unde bilan bolinishi kerak

item.title: Giá Sơn Mâm Xe Máy Lấy Nhanh Trong Ngày Tại TPHCM | Giá Sơn Mâm Xe Máy Lấy Nhanh Trong Ngày Tại TPHCM
item.description:
网站：sonxe168。 电子邮件： sonxe168@gmail.com



原文:

Sơn xe giá rất phải chăng – giải pháp sơn mâm xe gắn máy được đánh giá cao, chất lượng tối ưu tại thành phố hồ chí minh. Xin đề cử đến khách bảng ước tính kinh phí sơn mâm xe gắn máy chi phí rẻ đua tranh cả nước. Với đa sắc sơn mâm phong phú đổi mới các bảng màu nổi tiếng nhất mới ở thời điểm hiện tại. Nhằm chấp thuận với yêu sách giải pháp ở thời điểm hiện tại.
xem bài viết: https://sonxe168.vn/gia-son-mam-xe-may/
Tham khảo bảng giá sơn xe máy tphcm tại: https://sonxe168.vn/bang-gia-son-xe-may-tphcm/

Sơn xe giá rất phải chăng là một khu vực sơn mâm xe gắn máy mức giá rất mềm tại tp hồ chí minh. được nhiều sự đồng tình của những tín đồ mê đắm xe. Chọn là địa điểm sơn mâm xe gắn máy trong ngày đặc sắc tạo ra sự thẩm mĩ cho xe gắn máy.

Bạn muốn thay màu sơn khác mâm theo cung cách riêng của bản thân. Phù hợp với mẫu xe hay thời thượng ngày nay. Thì bạn hãy tới makeup nhé.

Việc sơn mâm không phải lo thủ tục như sơn đổi màu xe gắn máy. Nên bạn nhiều khả năng nhàn nhã đến thủ phủ để thay màu sơn khác mâm xe theo sở thích , độc đáo riêng của bản thân.

Nhiều kiểu màu trang nhã độc đáo hay là mạnh mẽ rõ nét thể thao. Bọn tôi sẽ phục vụ cho bạn yêu cầu đó. Còn gì vui thích hơn lúc chạy một con xe với đơn sắc sơn mâm đặc sắc theo ý vui thích.

Quyền lợi kể từ khi sơn mâm tại Sơn xe 168 Paint

Quyền lợi lúc khách đến sơn bánh mâm xe , chỉ dẩn cách bảo quàn màu sơn được bền lâu Sơn mâm xe tĩnh điện có tài năng chống gặm mòn cao bởi chất hóa học hoặc bị tác động của ảnh hưởng hóa học hay khí hậu ... đa sắc màu da dạng đa dạng , chuẩn màu độ chuẩn xác triệt để , độ bóng trong như gương Thi hành bởi kỹ thuật sơn tĩnh điện mâm xe gắn máy hàng đầu. Thời kỳ hoàn tất rút ngắn lại. Ra về trong ngày được nhân sự hướng dẩn cách bảo quản màu sơn, cam kết chất lượng và sửa chữa nếu xảy ra sự cố 2 đến 3 năm Kỹ thuật sơn tĩnh điện mâm xe tại Sơn xe 168 Paint Kỹ thuật sơn tĩnh điện dùng nguyên lý điện tử để định hình nên sự bám dính cho màng sơn. Lớp sơn sẽ được bọc lên trên bên ngoài chất liệu bằng một loại súng phun sơn.

Xem thêm: https://top247.vn/son-xe-air-blade-cac-doi-gia-tot-tai-tphcm/

Nổi bật để lúc bột sơn ngang qua súng sẽ được hâm nóng và tích điện dương ( + ) tại đầu kim phun. Về sau ngang qua kim phun và lưu thông theo điện trường để đến tới chất liệu sơn đã tích điện âm ( - ). Thời điểm hiện tại, dựa vào lực hút giữa các ion điện tích, bột sơn từng bước một bám vào quanh chất liệu sơn.

Cách thức này hỗ trợ bột sơn được rải đều quanh chất liệu, và có khả năng di chuyển vào đại đa số các phía bên ngoài bị khuất.

Cách sơn tĩnh điện mâm xe máy

Khi vừa tháo mâm thoát khỏi xe bọn tôi sẽ xúc tiến các bước theo phương pháp :

Bước 1 : vệ sinh phía bên ngoài cần sơn Bước 2 : tổ chức phun sơn Bước 3 : sau một thời gian phơi khô bề mặt Hứa giải pháp sơn mâm xe gắn máy giá trị tại Sơn xe 168 Paint Chuỗi vật dụng, công cụ toàn bộ, tối tân nhóm chuyên viên, thợ sơn xe bài bản, thâm niên nghề nghiệp. Bảng ước tính kinh phí được niêm yết công khai điều tra, tham mưu và đề xuất giải pháp tối ưu nhất cho mọi người. Luôn có lượng người đông và nhận được nhiều phẩm bình tốt của mọi người. Phân phối giấy hứa lúc để xe tại shop sơn. Cam kết chất lượng và sửa chữa nếu xảy ra sự cố cho các giải pháp hai năm. Với định hướng chu đáo, cẩn thận, uy tín

item.title: 手动测试与测试自动化 | Manual testing vs. Testing automation
item.description:
测试的重要性是任何软件项目不可或缺的一部分，因为它取决于各种因素，例如项目范围、预算、工作的复杂性和团队的专业知识。

测试过程的执行计划，特别是手动或自动测试之间的选择，是有助于项目整体成功的过程的基础。

由于自动化已成为测试过程的标准部分，它帮助公司利用新功能并提高工作质量。 它还提高了他们的 QA 和开发人员的生产力，同时跟上设备和操作系统激增带来的变化。 但是，它也带来了手动测试方式的改变。

有多种类型的测试，其中一些在自动化方面表现更好，而另一些则通过手动测试获得理想的结果。

在本文中，我们将仔细研究每种类型的测试，并最终比较手动与自动化测试。

什么是手动测试？

手动测试是一个涉及手动测试软件以确保各种功能正常工作的过程。 这种方法通常由最终用户（QA 分析师）执行，他们具有需求和正在测试的特性的角度。

在手动测试过程中，测试人员需要将他们遇到的任何问题报告给开发人员，然后开发人员需要修复这些问题。 此步骤通常发生在开发阶段，因为团队面临立即交付新功能的压力。

手动测试的类型

黑盒测试——一种软件测试方法，允许测试人员在不查看其内部代码结构的情况下评估软件的功能。 此方法可用于各种级别的软件测试，例如单元、集成和系统验收。

白盒测试——一种使用代码内部结构来评估其功能的软件测试。 它通常在单元级别进行。 这种类型的测试通常是在编程技能和内部视角的帮助下完成的。

单元测试- 也称为组件测试或模块测试。 此类测试由开发人员在开发环境中进行，以确保软件的各个组件正常工作。

系统测试- 称为端到端测试过程。 它涉及测试完全集成的软件，以确保其按照要求正常工作。

集成测试——测试过程以三种不同的方式进行。 大爆炸方法、自上而下的方法和自下而上的方法是一些常用的方法。

验收测试——生产前测试过程由最终用户和开发人员进行，以确保软件满足客户的要求。 验收测试成功后，进行正式测试，以确定软件是否按照客户的要求开发。

何时选择手动测试？

  1. 在验证阶段对软件的初始测试通常是手动完成的。 此步骤可确保正确执行测试过程并且发布候选版本正常工作。
  2. 在软件的支持阶段，会发布多个版本。 虽然自动化通常用于执行重复测试，但也并非总是可以多次执行相同的测试而不会遇到P1 错误。 在这种情况下，通常会进行手动测试以确保正确执行修复。
  3. 因此，在可用性测试期间，此测试的目标是分析软件的行为以及最终用户对它的感受。 由于需要人类智能，此过程是手动执行的。 由于人工智能和机器学习的进步，情绪测试也变得更加自动化。
  4. 在随机测试期间，这个过程被称为猴子测试，由开发人员和测试人员执行，以确保正确测试软件的各种功能。 由于没有定义的测试路径，测试结果是手动执行的。
  5. 有时，特定场景需要手动测试以确保软件正常工作。 例如，在集成环境中，门禁系统需要与软件交互。 由于传输和接收信息造成的延迟，此过程可能需要一些时间。 在这里您可以找到手动测试清单：

什么是自动化测试？

通过自动化软件测试，测试人员编写测试脚本，然后由软件开发人员进行验证。 他们使用自动化工具在相对较短的时间内完成测试执行。

自动化测试过程仅使用自动执行的预先编写的测试。 这确保了测试人员可以轻松地将测试结果与预期结果进行比较。

与手动测试不同，自动化测试不需要测试人员手动创建测试脚本。 它还可以在不影响工作质量的情况下执行重复性任务和回归测试。

自动化测试有利于现代交付； 它不是免费的。 为了充分利用它，团队需要确保他们拥有必要的工具和资源。 这可以通过创建测试脚本、数据验证和拆卸步骤来完成。

为什么选择自动化？

可以运行它们以确保新功能不会影响现有功能。 此外，自动化测试可以减少每次所需的监督量。

  1. 当你有时间限制时。 公司在开发软件时需要考虑的最重要因素之一是时间限制。 如果一个团队没有按时完成项目，那么它就会失去客户。
  2. 维护软件的成本也是公司在开发软件时必须考虑的一个主要问题。 拥有适当的自动化工具可以帮助防止代价高昂的维护问题。
  3. 自动化测试有益的最常见原因之一是软件中有多个配置选项可用。 这允许团队测试不同的功能和配置，而无需手动运行相同的测试用例。
  4. 另一个因素是安全测试。 拥有适当的自动化工具可以帮助防止各种攻击，例如拒绝服务攻击、跨站点脚本和模拟攻击。

自动化测试有利于敏捷开发团队提供的持续反馈，这对他们来说非常重要，因为它允许他们持续监控日常工作的质量。 它可以与夜间构建和按需版本一起执行。 持续测试也在公司持续开发管道的框架内执行。

手动测试和自动化测试有什么区别？

自动化和手动测试之间最大的区别在于谁编写测试用例。 在手动测试中，人类会这样做，而在自动化测试中，特殊工具会这样做。 从这个事实可以得出进一步的差异。 在下表中考虑它们：

比较手动测试与自动测试

手动或自动测试哪个更好？

没有正确的方法来回答手动测试还是自动测试更好的问题。 这两种方法各有优缺点，应在一定条件下使用。

虽然这两种方法都可以提供出色的结果，但在大多数情况下，只有两者结合才能提供最佳结果。 例如，如果您正在处理一个复杂的系统，那么使用自动化测试套件是不够的。 但是，如果您正在对小型产品进行测试，则使用手动测试过程仍然很重要。

除了这些之外，还应考虑其他因素，例如时间框架、团队的技能和预算，以确定自动化或手动测试是否适合您的项目。

自动化测试仪可以进行手动测试吗？

虽然自动化涉及替代手工工作，但在测试的情况下，手工测试的知识仍然是必要的。 这是有客观原因的。 手动测试过程可以帮助确定项目的哪个部分需要自动化。 它还可以帮助确定哪些测试输入和方法在给定场景中最有效。

如果您打开 QA 自动化测试员的当前职位空缺，您会看到职责会说，例如 - “分析和验证最佳自动化和手动测试方法”。 因此，专业的自动化测试人员可以进行手动测试。

总结

有很多人对软件测试感兴趣，他们中的许多人将不得不决定使用哪种类型的测试。 自动化或手动测试的真正价值来自于测试环境的选择。

在手动测试中，测试由人工执行。 此方法通常无需使用工具或脚本即可完成。 它非常耗时，并且不需要有关编程语言或其他相关主题的知识。

在自动化测试中，测试是在软件和脚本的帮助下执行的。 这种方法的目标是在更短的时间内完成测试。 但是，即使在这种情况下，也需要手动创建必要的测试脚本。

在选择自动测试还是手动测试时，没有正确的答案。 这两种方法通常适用于不同的项目，并且需要不同的因素，例如预算、时间和专业知识。 在选择自动或手动测试方法之前，了解这两种方法之间的区别很重要。

之前在maddevs.io/blog上发布。



原文:


The importance of testing is an integral part of any software project, as it depends on various factors such as the scope of the project, the budget, the complexity of the work, and the expertise of the team.

The execution plan for the testing process, in particular the choice between manual or automated testing, is the basis for a process that contributes to the overall success of the project.

Since automation has become a standard part of the testing process, it has helped companies take advantage of new features and improve the quality of their work. It has also increased the productivity of their QA and developers while keeping up with the changes brought about by the proliferation of devices and operating systems. However, it has also brought about a change in the way manual testing is done.

There are various types of testing and some of them perform better with automation, others get desirable results with manual testing.

In this article, we will look closer at each type of testing and eventually compare manual vs. automation testing.

What is Manual Testing?

Manual testing is a process that involves testing the software manually to ensure that the various features are working properly. This method is usually carried out by the end-user (QA Analyst), who has the perspective of the requirements and the features that are being tested.

During the manual testing process, the tester is required to report any issues that they come across to the developers, who then need to fix these problems. This step usually takes place during the development phase, as teams are under pressure to deliver new features immediately.

Types of manual testings

Black Box Testing - a software testing method that allows testers to evaluate the functionality of the software without looking at its internal code structure. This method can be utilized for various levels of software testing, such as Unit, Integration, and System Acceptance.

White Box Testing - a type of software testing that uses the internal structure of a code to evaluate its functionality. It is usually carried out at the unit level. This type of testing is usually done with the help of programming skills and an internal perspective.

Unit Testing - also known as component testing or module testing. This type of testing is carried out by the developers in the development environment to ensure that the various components of the software are working properly.

System Testing - is referred to as the end-to-end testing process. It involves testing the fully integrated software to ensure that it works properly according to the requirements.

Integration Testing - the testing process is carried out in three different ways. The Big Bang approach, the top-down approach, and the bottom-up approach are some of the methods that are commonly utilized.

Acceptance Testing - the pre-production testing process is conducted by the end users and the developers to ensure that the software meets the requirements of the customers. After successful acceptance tests, formal testing is carried out to determine if the software has been developed according to the requirements of the customers.

When to choose manual testing?

  1. The initial testing of software during the verification phase is usually done manually. This step ensures that the testing process is carried out properly and that the release candidate is working properly.
  2. During the support phase of software, multiple releases are made. While automation is commonly used to perform repetitive testing, it is also not always possible to perform the same tests multiple times without encountering a P1 bug. In such cases, manual testing is usually carried out to ensure that the fixes are performed properly.
  3. During usability testing, therefore the goal of this testing is to analyze the software's behavior and how it feels to the end-users. This process is carried out manually due to the need for human intelligence. Due to the advancement of AI and machine learning, the testing of emotion is also becoming more automated.
  4. During random testing, this process, which is referred to as monkey testing, is performed by the developers and testers to ensure that the software's various features are tested properly. Since there is no defined path for testing, the results of the tests are performed manually.
  5. Sometimes, specific scenarios require manual testing to ensure that the software works properly. For instance, in an integration environment, a gate system needs to interact with the software. This process can take a bit of time due to the delay caused by transmitting and receiving information. Here you can find the Manual testing checklist:

What is Automation Testing?

Through automated software testing, the testers write test scripts that are then validated by the software's developers. They use automation tools to complete the test execution in a relatively short time.

The automated testing process only uses pre-written tests that are performed automatically. This ensures that the testers can easily compare the results of the tests with the expected ones.

Unlike manual testing, automated testing does not require the testers to manually create the test scripts. It can also perform repetitive tasks and regression tests without affecting the quality of the work.

Automated testing is beneficial in modern-day delivery; it does not come at a free price. In order to get the most out of it, a team needs to make sure that they have the necessary tools and resources in place. This can be done through the creation of test scripts, data verification, and tear-down steps.

Why Automation?

1.If there is a large number of repetitive tests to be run, then automated tests are the best option. They can be run in order to ensure that the new features don't affect the existing ones. Also, automating a test can reduce the amount of supervision needed each time.

  1. When you have time limitations. One of the most important factors that a company needs to consider when it comes to developing software is time constraints. If a team doesn't finish the project on time, then it loses customers.
  2. The cost of maintaining the software is also a major issue that a company has to consider when it comes to developing software. Having the proper automation tools can help prevent costly maintenance issues.
  3. One of the most common reasons why automated testing is beneficial is when multiple configuration options are available in the software. This allows the team to test different features and configurations without having to manually run the same test cases.
  4. Another factor is the security tests. Having the proper automation tools can help prevent various kinds of attacks, such as denial of service attacks, cross-site scripting, and impersonation attacks.

Automated testings are beneficial for the continuous feedback that agile development teams provide is very important for them as it allows them to continuously monitor the quality of their daily work. It can be executed along with nightly builds and on-demand versions. Continuous testing is also executed within the framework of the company's continuous development pipeline.

What is the difference between manual and automation testing?

The biggest difference between automated and manual testing is who writes the test case. In manual testing the human does it, and in automation testing special tools does. From this fact come further differences. Consider them in the table below:

Comparison Manual testing vs. Automated testing

Which is better manual or automated testing?

There is no right way to answer the question of whether manual or automated testing is better. Both of these approaches have their advantages and disadvantages, and they should be used under certain conditions.

Although both approaches can provide excellent results, in most cases, only a combination of both can deliver the best results. For instance, if you are working on a complex system, using an automated test suite is not enough. However, if you are testing on a small product, using a manual testing process is still important.

Aside from these, other factors such as the timeframe, team's skillset, and budget should also be taken into account to determine if automated or manual testing is the right approach for your project.

Can automation Tester do manual testing?

Although automation involves replacing manual work, in the case of testing, knowledge of manual testing is still necessary. And there is an objective reason for this. The manual testing process can help determine which part of the project needs to be automated. It can also help determine which test inputs and methods would work best in the given scenario.

If you open the current job vacancies for the QA automation tester, you will see that the responsibilities will say, for example - "Analyze and verify best automated and manual test approaches". Accordingly, a professional automated tester can do manual tests.

To wrap up

There are many people who are interested in software testing, and many of them will have to make a decision about which type of testing to use. The real value of automated or manual testing comes from the choice of the testing environment.

In manual testing, the tests are performed by humans. This method is usually done without the use of tools or scripts. It is very time-consuming and doesn't require knowledge about programming languages or other related topics.

In automated testing, the tests are performed with the help of software and scripts. The goal of this method is to complete the test in a shorter amount of time. However, even in this case, manual efforts are required to create the necessary testing scripts.

There is no right answer when it comes to choosing between automated or manual testing. The two methods are usually applicable to different projects and require different factors such as the budget, timeline, and expertise. Before choosing an automated or manual testing method, it is important to understand the difference between these two methods.

Previously published at maddevs.io/blog.

item.title: 无服务器架构 | Serverless Architecture
item.description:
您有没有想过 AWS Lambda 函数或 Google 的 Cloud Functions 或其他函数即服务如何工作？ 还是无服务器架构真的无服务器？ 或者第三方服务如何在容器中管理您完整的后端服务代码？

在这篇文章中，我们将了解无服务器架构及其实际工作原理。 我们还将探索这个问题的答案，无服务器架构真的是无服务器的吗？ 我们还将探索 AWS、GCP 等不同的云提供商如何为我们提供这种架构。 我们还将了解什么是 FaaS（功能即服务）以及我们如何使用这些云提供商中的任何一个来实际实施 FaaS。

为了更好地理解这篇文章，我真的建议你阅读我之前的一篇关于“ Web 应用架构基础”的文章，如果你想了解更多关于云的知识，请查看“云计算”。 如果您已经了解 Web 的工作原理以及一些不同的常用 Web 架构，请随时继续前进。

介绍

无服务器架构是现代 Web 架构形式之一，我们在其中部署应用程序，其中包含管理服务器配置、负载平衡和其他服务器端任务的第三方服务。

后端需要提供的服务的分布有点类似于微服务架构。 就像在微服务架构中，后端分布在不同的服务中，每个服务用于不同的任务，在无服务器架构中（尤其是在 FaaS 中），后端被划分为不同的任务，这些任务中的每一个都可以实现为单独和单独的功能。 然后，这些函数通过不同端点的 API 提供服务，并且可以由前端访问。

这就是基于无服务器架构构建的基本应用程序的样子。

功能即服务 (FaaS)

在这个实现中，我们创建了多个函数来执行每个任务，这些任务是后端服务器逻辑或服务的独立部分。

让我们考虑 AWS Lambda 函数的案例来理解这一点。

这些函数作为容器部署在服务器上，每台服务器上可以运行多个函数实例，具体取决于函数所需的用户流量和计算能力。

这些非常容易构建，AWS 允许您集成许多其他服务，如 DynamoDB（AWS NoSQL 数据库）、S3（存储服务）、SQS（排队系统）、SNS（通知系统）等。

用例和限制

现在让我们看看一些不同的情况，我们可以在哪些情况下实际使用无服务器功能，以及它将如何对我们有益，或者可能存在哪些限制。

数据处理或收集

您可以创建一个非常小的函数，仅将用户提交的 Web 应用程序的表单数据存储到 DynamoDB（数据库），然后使用该数据进行自我分析。

后端逻辑（客户端重应用）

由于对于客户端繁重的应用程序，后端非常轻量级并且具有高延迟，因此后端或服务器端逻辑很容易在 lambda 函数中实现，并且由于您不必管理部署，它变得有点类似于后端即服务 (BaaS)，基本上是为您提供后端而无需您管理它的平台。 您无需了解 CI/CD 管道等概念或其他与 DevOps 相关的概念即可扩展或部署您的逻辑。

客户端繁重的应用程序可能包括个人博客、设计应用程序或任何其他大部分逻辑都通过前端实现的应用程序。

限制

当 lambda 函数闲置一段时间后，它们需要经历冷启动，这意味着启动和响应需要时间。 因此，如果需要快速响应，并且可能会出现预期的低流量时段，那么 lambda 函数就不太适合。 此外，当应用程序具有繁重的后端，需要大量计算能力时，理想情况下我们不应该使用 lambda 函数，因为它们在这些用例中效率不高。

例如，您不应将 Lambda 函数用于MLOps（ML 模型部署） ，因为它们可能需要非常高的计算量并且具有高延迟。

结论

无服务器架构是一个很好的框架，可以在您的应用程序中实现。 凭借其众多优点，它也有一些局限性，因此作为最佳实践，您不应仅仅依赖 lambda 函数作为后端，而应开发一个混合后端，由无服务器函数实现轻量级函数。 这不仅可以减少您管理服务器逻辑的负担，还可以让您更加专注于您打算提供的主要产品或服务。

现在您了解了无服务器架构的基本功能，我们可以继续了解云计算和架构的更多细节。 我们可以探索其他形式的架构，并了解这些云功能如何单独工作。

为了支持我的文章，您可以在Thinkfeed上查看我的官方博客并订阅那里的时事通讯，这样您就不会错过任何帖子并保持自己的更新。 享受阅读！

本文最初发表于https://thinkfeed.divyesshm.com/post/serverless-architecture

戴维德·德·保利斯—— Jan 25 1月25日



原文:

Have you ever wondered how the AWS Lambda functions or Cloud Functions by Google or other Functions as a Service work? or is serverless architecture really serverless? or how a third-party service manages your complete backend service code in containers?

In this post, we are going to understand the serverless architecture and how it actually works. We are also going to explore the answer to the question, is serverless architecture really serverless? and we are also going to explore how this architecture is provided in handy to us by different cloud providers like AWS, GCP, and many others. We will also take a look at what is FaaS (Function as a Service) and how we can actually implement a FaaS using any of these cloud providers.

To have a better understanding of this article, I really recommend you to read one of my previous articles on “Basics of Web App Architecture” and if you want to know more about the cloud, check out “Cloud Computing”. In case you already have an understanding of how the web works and what are some different commonly used web architectures, feel free to move ahead.

Introduction

Serverless architecture is one of the modern forms of web architecture where we deploy our application that incorporates the third-party services that manage the server configurations, load balancing, and other server-side tasks.

The distribution of the services that need to be provided in the backend is somewhat similar to the microservice architecture. Just like in microservice architecture, the backend is distributed among different services, each of them being used for different tasks, in serverless architecture (especially in FaaS), the backend is divided into different tasks, and each of these tasks can be implemented as a separate and individual function. These functions are then served through an API over different endpoints and can be accessed by the frontend.

This is what a basic application built over serverless architecture would look like.

Function as a Service (FaaS)

FaaS is the most popular and newer implementation of serverless architecture. In this implementation, we create multiple functions to perform each task which is an independent part of the backend server logic or service.

Let us consider the case of AWS Lambda functions to understand this.

“AWS Lambda is a serverless, event-driven compute service that lets you run code for virtually any type of application or backend service without provisioning or managing servers.” ~ https://aws.amazon.com/lambda/

The lambda functions do not require you to manage their deployment or load balancing yourself, and rather the infrastructure of AWS manages that for you. These functions are deployed as containers on the server, where each server can have multiple function instances running on it depending upon the user traffic and computation power required by the functions.

These are very easy to build and AWS allows you to integrate many other services like DynamoDB ( AWS NoSQL Database ), S3 (Storage Service), SQS (Queuing System), SNS (Notification System), and so on.

Lambda functions are not only easy to build and deploy but they are also very cost effective since you are charged on the basis of computation time used by the function instances, which is only accounted for when the function is called. AWS even provides you with about 1 million free requests and 3.2 million seconds of compute time per month.

Use Cases and Limitations

Now let us have a look at some different cases where we can actually use the serverless functions and how it will be beneficial for us, or what can be some of the limitations.

Data Processing or Collection

Lambda functions can be great for the lightweight processing of data for an application since they generally do not take much compute time and it will be very easy to store the data using other services. You can create a very small function to just store the form data of a web application submitted by a user to DynamoDB (Database), and later use that data for self-analysis.

Backend Logic (CLient Heavy Application)

Since for the client-heavy applications, the backend is very lightweight and has high latency, the backend or server side logic is very easy to be implemented in the lambda functions and since you do not have to manage your deployment, it becomes somewhat similar to Backend as a Service (BaaS), which basically are platforms that provide you with backend without you requiring to manage it. You will not need to know concepts like the CI/CD pipelines or other DevOps-related concepts to scale or deploy your logic.

The client heavy applications might include a personal blog, a designing application, or any other application where a good part of logic is implemented over frontend.

Limitations

When a lambda function is left idle for some time, they are required to go through a cold start which means it takes time to start and respond. So if a quick response is needed, and there can be an expected low traffic period, then lambda functions are not very suitable. Also when the applications have a heavy backend, which would require a lot of computation power, then we should ideally not use lambda functions, since they are not efficient in those use cases.

For example, you should not use Lambda functions for MLOps ( Deployment of ML Models ), since they can require very high computation and have high latency.

Conclusion

Serverless architecture is a great framework to be implemented in your applications. With its many advantages, it also has some limitations, and hence as a best practice, you should not depend solely on lambda functions for your backend, and instead develop a hybrid backend, with the lightweight functions getting implemented by the serverless functions. This will not only reduce loads of you for management of the server logic but also let you focus more on the main product or service that you intend to provide.

Now that you understand the basic function of serverless architectures, we can move on to understanding more details of cloud computing and architecture. We can explore other forms of architecture as well as understand how each of these cloud functionality works individually.

To support my articles, you can check out my official blog at Thinkfeed and subscribe to the newsletter over there so that you never miss a post and keep yourself updated. Enjoy Reading!

This article was originally published on https://thinkfeed.divyesshm.com/post/serverless-architecture

Davide de Paolis - Jan 25

item.title: 在 RabbitMQ 上读取消息的简单工具 | Simple tool to read messages on RabbitMQ
item.description:
达努什卡·马杜山

发表于我找到了以下存储库，可让您轻松阅读 RabbitMQ 消息。
这会将代码编译到$GOPATH/bin中，您可以从那里使用该工具。 以下是一个示例命令，您可以从 rabbitmq 中转储多达 500 条消息及其标头数据：

 ./rabbitmq-dump-queue -uri="amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672/" -queue=queue1 -max-messages=500 -output-dir=/output/locaiton -full

塞缪尔·欣奇利夫🚀 - May 4 5月4日



原文:

Dhanushka madushan

Posted on May 5

I found the following repository that let you to read RabbitMQ messages easily.
https://github.com/dubek/rabbitmq-dump-queue This is Go lang based tool and you can use it with the following command:

go get github.com/dubek/rabbitmq-dump-queue

This will compile the code into the $GOPATH/bin and you can use the tool from there. Following is a sample command that you can dump upto 500 messages from the rabbitmq along with its header data:

./rabbitmq-dump-queue  -uri="amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672/" -queue=queue1 -max-messages=500 -output-dir=/output/locaiton -full

Samuel Hinchliffe 🚀 - May 4
